citeinfo.title: Water quality, nutrient, and phytoplankton data from Franks Tract, Mildred Island, and neighboring channels in the California Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta: 2022 and 2023 High-resolution mapping surveys

descript.abstract: Surface water quality, water isotope, and phytoplankton enumeration data were collected to evaluate the impact of an Emergency Drought Barrier (EDB) in False River in the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta. Data were collected on six days during 2022 and 2023 (June 7, June 21, July 27, August 9, October 12, November 30, 2022, and February 21, 2023). Sampling occurred in Franks Tract, Mildred Island, the San Joaquin River, and various sloughs and cuts connecting these water bodies. High-resolution boat-based mapping data and discrete water samples were collected and analyzed for: nitrate, nitrite, ammonium, soluble reactive phosphorus (orthophosphate), total dissolved nitrogen, total dissolved phosphorus, total phosphorus, total particulate carbon and nitrogen, water isotopes (2H and 18O), dissolved organic carbon, chlorophyll a, and phytoplankton enumeration (microscopy).
descript.purpose: During the 2020-2022 California drought, the California Department of Water Resources installed an emergency drought barrier (EDB) in west False River in the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta (Delta). The EDB blocks water flow through west False River, which prevents higher salinity water from entering the central Delta via Franks Tract. Preventing saltwater from intruding into the central Delta is essential because a large portion of the state’s fresh water exported from the Delta for drinking water, agricultural and urban transits through this region. While preventing saltwater intrusion into Franks Tract, the EDB also changes the hydrology of the region, which may alter water residence times and the phytoplankton community in Franks Tract. Water quality sampling was conducted during and following the installation of the EDB to help evaluate its impact on water residence time, water quality, and phytoplankton.
descript.supplinf: See file: "FranksTract_2022-2023_DataDictionary.xlsx" for further information regarding the datasets.

idinfo.accconst: None. Please see 'Distribution Info' for details.
idinfo.useconst: None. Users are advised to read the dataset's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.

citeinfo.title: EXO User Manual
BAM, C-Star, C-Rover Transmissometers User Manual

attracc.attraccr: EXO sondes were calibrated before use in the field with the KorEXO software. For calibration, the sonde sensors are submerged one at a time in standards, then the verified chemical concentration is recorded within the instrument. If the instrument measures an unknown value of the parameter, it refers to the calibration information to calculate the concentration of the chemical in the water (EXO User Manual, pg. 81). Some software may interpret blank values as zero. Blank values should be used with caution. Values given as "NA" are not available. Typically, this means a given parameter was not analyzed for a given discrete water quality sample, or there is no associated remark code.

dataqual.logic: Redundancy is built into the system, meaning many of the parameters included in the data set are measured by 2 different instruments. Data was displayed in real-time for on the spot user verification. Topology rules were applied to points, lines, and polygons to ensure the integrity of the geospatial data.
dataqual.complete: Data set is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: We conducted a visual check that the GPS data we recorded matched the boat GPS. Data was also displayed in real-time, allowing us to spot check GPS data underway. In addition, GPS location information was compared to field notes that described known locations and features. Datum used NAD1983.

procstep.procdesc:
HIGH RESOLUTION DATA COLLECTION:
Data was collected by boat (USGS R/V Aiken) in Franks Tract and Mildred Island, following the approach described by Downing and others (2016), Fichot and others (2016), Kimmerer and others (2019) and Stumpner and others (2020). Transects were established in each flooded island, three at Franks Tract (FT1-3) and two at Mildred Island (MI1-2). Sample water was continuously pumped onto the boat using two SHURflo Aqua King II pumps at a pressure of 60 psi and rate of 5 GPM (SHURflo Pentair, London, United Kingdom) while underway at speeds up to 30 miles per hour (mph) (13 meters per second [m/s]), using a pickup tube mounted at a fixed depth of approximately 1 meter below the surface. Water was routed through a 178-micrometer (um) in-line strainer (Cole Parmer; EW-29595-47, Vernon Hills, IL) to remove large debris and into a pressure-compensated manifold that maintained system pressure (~20 PSI) at a prescribed level irrespective of boat speed (Downing and others, 2016). A two-stage debubbler was used to remove bubbles that can interfere with optical measurements. Sample water was split into separated analytical flow paths, with constant flow rates maintained by metering the discharge line, except for the flow path serving the open split interface, which was metered on the feed line. Flows were continuously monitored using a sight gauge (0.1-1 GPM Flow Meter LZM-15 with adjustable knob) for each flow path.
All instrumentation was cleaned, and calibrations were checked prior to each use following the manufacturer's recommendation or as described below. Data for most instruments were recorded at 1-second frequency on a single data logger (CR6, Campbell Scientific, Logan, Utah, United States) together with a timestamp and boat position obtained from a high-resolution global positioning system receiver (16X-HVS, Garmin, Olathe, Kansas). The FluoroProbe logged data internally and to the host software every 1-2 seconds. The NH4 analyzer was connected to a stand-alone computer and collected data through its native software. The isotopic water sampler collected and logged data through its native software to an internal computer. All data were displayed in real time, so scientists on board could respond when they observed changes relevant to study objectives, or if issues with flow or instrument performance were noted. Data collected in situ are referred to as high resolution (HR) parameters.
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/64ee7f82d34e4b6d8ab71438).

DISCRETE WATER SAMPLING:
Discrete water samples (DS) were collected both while stopped at station, and while underway. Samples were collected from designated sampling stations at 25 sites in Franks Tract and Mildred Island, and 17 other established sites. Filtered samples were collected from approximately 1 m depth by passing water from the pickup tube through a 0.2-micron filter into a sample bottle. Filtered samples were collected for the analysis of ammonium (NH4), nitrate and nitrite (NO3 + NO2), soluble reactive phosphorus (SRP, PO4), dissolved organic nitrogen (DON), total dissolved nitrogen (TDN), total dissolved phosphorus (TDP), dissolved organic carbon (DOC), and water-stable isotopes (2H and 18O). Filtered samples were collected using this system for 24 underway samples. Unfiltered samples were collected from approximately 1 m depth using a submersible pump for analysis of chlorophyll a (chl a), pheophytin, phytoplankton enumeration, total phosphorus (TP), and total particulate carbon and nitrogen (TPCN). Samples for phytoplankton enumeration counts were fixed immediately with Lugol’s iodine solution (~5%).
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/64ee7f82d34e4b6d8ab71438).

DATA PROCESSING:
Data from onboard continuous instruments not directly logged to the flow-through data collection system were merged based on timestamp to the nearest second after estimating the differential travel times between flow paths. The time differential arises from lag introduced by filtering and specifics of instrument operation. Examples include the Picarro water isotope analyzer. All data, except that collected by the Picarro, was processed using the pandas software library (0.220; McKinney, 2010) in Python (3.7.3; Van Rossum and Drake, 2009) to remove periods of compromised data (e.g. flow blockages, bubbles), to apply instrument corrections and unit conversions, and to apply a centered 20 second median filter to the time series. Final NO3 values were obtained by regressing instrument response against NO3 concentrations obtained from laboratory measurements of discrete samples collected through the course of the day. Individual sample results greater than three standard deviations from the regression were judged to be outliers and removed from the regression (Pellerin and others, 2013).
More detailed methods documentation can be found on the landing page (https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/64ee7f82d34e4b6d8ab71438).

enttyp.enttypd: Definition of column headers, and data offsets and corrections for Franks Tract 2022-2023 data release

overview.eaover: Column header definitions and data offsets and corrections are described in an .xlsx file: "FranksTract_2022-2023_DataDictionary.xlsx". More detailed description of sample collection, processing, and analysis are described in the methods document: "FranksTract_2022-2023_methods.docx" on the landing page. High resolution (HR), discrete (DS), and phytoenumeration (PE) files are also included.

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab: Unless otherwise stated, all data, metadata and related materials are considered to satisfy the quality standards relative to the purpose for which the data were collected. Although these data and associated metadata have been reviewed for accuracy and completeness and approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), no warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the display or utility of the data on any other system or for general or scientific purposes, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Biological Data Profile of the Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
